gene_description: HECT and RLD domains containing E3 ubiquitin protein ligase 2 (HERC2) — intron 86 contains regulatory element controlling OCA2 expression and eye pigmentation

source.title: Blue eye color in humans may be caused by a perfectly associated founder mutation in a regulatory element located within the HERC2 gene inhibiting OCA2 expression
source.finding: rs12913832 is the best-known predictor of blue vs brown eye color. Located 21.152 bp upstream of OCA2 promoter in HERC2 intron 86. G allele associated with blue eyes (~94% accuracy in Europeans) by reducing OCA2 expression.

GG.effect: Homozygous for G allele. Strongly associated with blue eye color. G allele is found in regulatory enhancer of HERC2 that suppresses OCA2 expression, reducing melanin production in the iris. ~94% of individuals with this genotype have blue eyes.
GG.recommendation: No clinical action needed. Blue eyes are more sensitive to light and sunglare; polarized sunglasses may improve comfort.

AG.effect: Heterozygous A/G genotype. Most commonly associated with brown eye color, though intermediate shades can occur. Permits higher OCA2 expression than GG genotype, resulting in increased melanin deposition.
AG.recommendation: No clinical action needed. Standard UV eye protection recommended for all individuals.

GA.effect: Heterozygous A/G genotype. Most commonly associated with brown eye color, though intermediate shades can occur. Permits higher OCA2 expression than GG genotype, resulting in increased melanin deposition.
GA.recommendation: No clinical action needed. Standard UV eye protection recommended for all individuals.

AA.effect: Homozygous for A allele (ancestral). Associated with brown eye color due to unrestricted OCA2 expression and high melanin production in the iris. Most common phenotype worldwide.
AA.recommendation: No clinical action needed. Standard UV eye protection recommended for all individuals.
